```{r cycle, results='asis'}
  else if (isTRUE(sp$AboveSMA200) && sp$ROC20 <= 0) "Distribution (topping) 🏔️"
  else if (!isTRUE(sp$AboveSMA200) && sp$ROC20 < 0) "Markdown (downtrend) 📉"
  else "Accumulation (basing) 🌱"
cat(sprintf("**Estimated cycle phase (S&P 500):** %s\n\n", phase))
cat(sprintf("**Fear/Greed gauge:** %d / 100.\n\n", fear_greed))
cat(sprintf("**Volatility:** VIX %.1f vs 60-day average %.1f.\n\n", vix_last, vix_avg))
```

```{r volume}
#| echo: false
#| results: asis
## Volume / low-liquidity check on S&P 500
volx <- Vo(market[["S&P 500"]])
if (!is.null(volx) && sum(as.numeric(volx), na.rm=TRUE) > 0) {
  vv <- as.numeric(volx); recent <- tail(vv,5); avg <- mean(tail(vv,60))
  cat(sprintf("Recent 5-day average volume is %.0f%% of the 60-day average — %s.",
      mean(recent)/avg*100,
      ifelse(mean(recent)/avg < 0.8, "unusually thin (low-conviction moves)","normal participation")))
}
```

Bid-ask spreads are not available on free feeds, so liquidity here is proxied by volume trends and by the VIX. The fear/greed gauge is built from three computable inputs — 20-day momentum, VIX versus its 60-day average, and the breadth of indices above their 200-day average — using only freely fetchable data.

# 🔮 Forecasts

```{r forecast}
#| echo: false
nm <- "S&P 500"; cl <- as.numeric(Cl(market[[nm]])); cl <- cl[!is.na(cl)]
fit <- tryCatch(forecast::auto.arima(tail(cl, 750)), error = function(e) NULL)
if (is.null(fit)) fit <- forecast::ets(tail(cl, 750))
fc <- forecast::forecast(fit, h = 252, level = c(80, 95))
hx <- tail(index(Cl(market[[nm]])), 750)
fdt <- next_bdays(max(hx), 252)
marks <- FC_STEPS
p <- plot_ly() |>
  add_lines(x = hx, y = tail(cl, 750), name = "History") |>
  add_ribbons(x = fdt, ymin = as.numeric(fc$lower[,2]),
              ymax = as.numeric(fc$upper[,2]),
              name = "95% band", opacity = 0.15, line = list(width = 0)) |>
  add_ribbons(x = fdt, ymin = as.numeric(fc$lower[,1]),
              ymax = as.numeric(fc$upper[,1]),
              name = "80% band", opacity = 0.25, line = list(width = 0)) |>
  add_lines(x = fdt, y = as.numeric(fc$mean), name = "Point forecast",
            line = list(dash = "dash", color = PAL$coral_deep)) |>
  add_markers(x = fdt[marks], y = as.numeric(fc$mean[marks]),
              name = "1w · 1m · 3m · 6m · 1y marks",
              marker = list(size = 9, color = "black"))
p |> pastel_layout(title = paste(nm, "— full-year fan chart with horizon marks (auto.arima). Watch the bands widen: that widening IS the forecast."),
            hovermode = "x unified")
```

```{r forecast-plain, results='asis'}
#| echo: false
## Translate the 95% bands into plain-probability sentences
lastp <- tail(cl, 1)
band_line <- function(step, label) {
  lo <- (as.numeric(fc$lower[step, 2]) / lastp - 1) * 100
  hi <- (as.numeric(fc$upper[step, 2]) / lastp - 1) * 100
  sprintf("- **%s from now** 🎲: 19 times out of 20, the model expects the S&P 500 to land somewhere between **%+.0f%%** and **%+.0f%%**.\n", label, lo, hi)
}
cat("**The same chart in plain probability language** (this is the honest version of every price target you'll ever read):\n\n")
cat(band_line(21,  "1 month"))
cat(band_line(63,  "3 months"))
cat(band_line(252, "1 year"))
cat("\nNotice how the 1-year range is wide enough to contain both a great year *and* a bad one at the same time. 🤷 That's not the model failing — that's the model being **honest**. Anyone quoting you a single confident 1-year number has simply deleted the error bars.\n")
```

```{r forecast-table}
#| echo: false
if (!is.null(fc_all)) {
  datatable(fc_all, rownames = FALSE,
            options = list(pageLength = 25, dom = "t"),
            caption = "All key-asset projections at every horizon (1 week to 1 year ahead). No 5-year forecasts — deliberately.") |>
    formatStyle("Chg %", color = styleInterval(0, c(PAL$coral_deep, PAL$seafoam_deep)))
}
```

**The reliability ladder** — how much to trust each row above:

- **1 week ahead** 🟢 — usable; mostly mean-reversion and momentum carry-over.
- **1 month ahead** 🟢 — usable; still anchored to current trend and volatility.
- **3 months ahead** 🟡 — soft; regime changes start to dominate model structure.
- **6 months ahead** 🟠 — indicative only; the 95% band typically spans an entire correction and recovery.
- **1 year ahead** 🔴 — directional at best; included because requested, trusted at your peril. 🐻 A year is long enough for the entire weather system to change twice.
- **5 years ahead** ⛔ — not produced. Univariate models at that range are astrology with confidence intervals.

Forecasts use `forecast::auto.arima()` with an `ets()` fallback — chosen over prophet to keep the document dependency-light and reproducible at any date. These are naive univariate extrapolations and must not be read as predictions.

# 📚 Methodology Appendix

- **Data cascade (reliability-first).** Every index *and every sector theme* is fetched through a chain of up to nine REAL sources, each network call retried 3x with backoff: (1–2) Yahoo v8 JSON on two independent hosts, (3) quantmod/Yahoo, (4) quantmod/Stooq, (5) direct Stooq CSV, (6) Tiingo (when an API key is configured), (7–8) a designated backup ETF for the same exposure via Yahoo and Stooq, (9) FRED official close-only series for the major indices. The winning source is recorded and shown in the dashboards. **Strict mode (default): if every real source fails for any asset, the render aborts with an error naming it — an incomplete or simulated report is never produced.** The simulated generator survives only behind `STRICT_DATA=false` for offline layout testing. Lookback is ~5.6 years so the 5-year horizon always resolves.
- **Time horizons.** Realized returns are computed over calendar windows (7/30/91/182/365/1826 days, using the last close on or before the cutoff). Forward projections read horizons off a *single* 252-step `auto.arima` forecast per key asset at 5/21/63/126/252 trading days — one fit, five readings, no re-estimation drift. **No 5-year-ahead forecast is produced**; the 1-year row is retained but explicitly labelled directional-at-best.
- **Bear Watch.** Fourteen binary warning lights (trend breaks, correction/bear thresholds, breadth < 50%, death crosses, VIX level and spike, negative momentum, near-high-but-weak-RSI divergence proxy, gold-over-stocks rotation, crypto risk-appetite drain, heat-score danger zone, yield-curve inversion, credit stress). Bear Risk Score = share of lights ON; alert text is pure if/else on that score. The executive summary always surfaces the active signals. The credit-stress light is suppressed (kept off) when either bond ETF fell back to simulated data.
- **Recession & Credit Radar.** (1) Yield curve = FRED DGS10 − DGS2, key-less; inversion (<0) trips a bear light. (2) Credit stress = HYG/LQD closing ratio through the same 5-source cascade; stress = ratio below its 200-day SMA *and* falling over 20 sessions. (3) Drawdown = close ÷ running maximum − 1, with −10%/−20% reference lines.
- **Historical odds ("What Are the Chances?").** Pure counting on the fetched S&P 500 closes: for each window length k ∈ {1, 5, 21, 63, 126, 252} trading days, the share of all k-day windows ending higher. Conditional odds split the forward-21-day return by the price-vs-200-day-SMA regime on the entry day. Overlapping windows are used (they are not independent samples — stated in the on-page footnote), and results are sample-specific to the ~5.6-year lookback.
- **Probability translations.** All 95% forecast intervals are additionally rendered as "19 times out of 20" plain-language ranges relative to the last close, so non-specialists read distributions instead of false point certainty.
- **Trend indicators (TTR):** 50/200-day SMAs, RSI(14), MACD(12,26,9) histogram, 20/60-day ROC, 52-week high/low distance, golden/death-cross detection, 50-day-MA slope.
- **Sector Trend Score (0–100):** 15 pts price>50d MA + 15 pts price>200d MA + 10 pts 50d>200d + 15 pts rising 50d MA + 15 pts scaled ROC20 + 20 pts scaled ROC60 + 10 pts proximity to 52-week high. Pure price behaviour, no valuation — deliberate for themes that trade on flows and narrative.
- **Relative strength:** 60-day change of the theme/S&P-500 price ratio.
- **Gold deep dive:** GLD candles with 50/200-day MAs and Bollinger(20,2); support/resistance = 60-session low/high extremes; RSI/MACD panels; gold/silver ETF ratio (trend-identical to the oz ratio); gold/S&P rebased ratio; US 10-year real yield from FRED (DGS10 − T10YIE, no API key needed) as gold's macro gravity; horizon returns and projections. All commentary is pre-written if/else on RSI zones, ratio-vs-average and trend score.
- **Composite heat score (0–100):** `0.35·CAPE + 0.20·P/E + 0.20·RSI + 0.25·breadth`, each sub-score min-max scaled to historical ranges; computed on world indices only.
- **Fear/greed gauge:** equal-weight blend of momentum, VIX-vs-average, and breadth.
- **Cycle phase:** simple rules on price-vs-200d MA and 20-day momentum (accumulation / markup / distribution / markdown).
- **Weather text:** pure if/else on the composite, the sector trend scores, the bear score, the gauge, VIX and breadth — the maths runs silently and only plain English with emojis is surfaced.

# ⚠️ Disclaimer

This report is generated automatically for **educational and illustrative purposes only**. It is **not financial advice**, not a recommendation to buy or sell any security or crypto-asset, and may contain errors or simulated data. Past performance and model forecasts do not guarantee future results. Consult a licensed professional before investing.
